title: cerastium vulgatum subsp. lucorum
intro: Cerastium vulgatum subsp. lucorum, also known as the Wood Crane's-bill or Alpine Whitlow Grass, is a plant species that belongs to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) and is characterized by its white or pink flowers and hairy leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations and wounds, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds, which have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. Cerastium vulgatum subsp. lucorum can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections.
